title: 中考六选五 6: The Trap of Looking Busy
passage:
Many students believe that being busy means being productive. They fill their notebooks, stay at their desks for hours and keep many study apps open. 1. ______ Real progress depends not on how busy you look, but on what you actually finish and understand.

A long to-do list can feel satisfying. Students may cross off easy tasks first, such as copying notes or organizing books. 2. ______ This creates a false sense of achievement, because the most important work is still waiting.

Another problem is switching tasks too often. A student may start with maths, then check a message, then open an English exercise. 3. ______ As a result, the mind becomes active but not deeply focused.

To avoid this trap, students should ask one simple question: What result matters most today? 4. ______ Sometimes finishing one difficult task is more valuable than doing five easy but less important ones.

Rest also matters. Studying for a long time without breaks may look hardworking, but tired thinking is often poor thinking. 5. ______ Smart study is not about looking busy; it is about making real progress.

questions.label: Questions 1-5 - Sentence Insertion
questions.instruction: Choose the best sentence from A-F for each blank in the passage. There is one extra sentence you do not need.

choices.text: However, activity is not always the same as progress.
The hardest task may remain untouched.
A clear goal helps them choose work that truly moves them forward.
Each switch leaves a small piece of attention behind.
Working longer is not useful if the mind is too tired to think well.
Students should always choose the quickest tasks first.
